name: Dashboard Internal Tool
professionalExpectations: An internal dashboard must answer operational questions quickly using trusted records and clear status indicators.
It should be dense, restrained, scannable, and built around repeated work rather than marketing presentation.
Read-only dashboard foundations must not imply write capability or live connector access.
Every displayed metric must have a known source, freshness expectation, and limitation.
minimumQualityBar: Source, status, timestamp, and uncertainty are visible for each important data area.
No live connector, database, production data, customer data, or deployment connection exists without approval.
Navigation, filtering, responsive layout, and empty states are clear.
Risk, approval, validation, boot, cost, and incident surfaces are not hidden.
commonModules: Health overview, status cards, registry summaries, project list, approval queue, cost panel, and incident panel.
Validation, boot, CI, connector, watchdog, and storage-risk status surfaces.
Audit trail, recent changes, blocked actions, and next required owner approval.
Read-only data adapters from repository records before live runtime adapters exist.
uxExpectations: Use compact tables, status chips, filters, and clear timestamps.
Avoid landing-page layout, oversized hero sections, or decorative cards inside cards.
Make blocked, stale, failed, and approval-needed states visually distinct.
Keep write actions absent until a separate governed command model exists.

components: Static dashboard generated from repository records
Netlify only after approved read-only deployment
Live adapters only after connector and data approval
recommendedStack.notes: Start read-only from existing records. Do not add live connectors or write controls in the first dashboard.

phases.name: Read-Only Visibility
goals: Display registry, project, cost, approval, boot, and watchdog status from repository data.
Validate build and dashboard checks locally.

phases.name: Operational Context
goals: Add filtering, source timestamps, limitations, and blocked-action explanations.
Expose what needs owner approval without creating write controls.

phases.name: Approved Runtime
goals: Add live adapters only after owner approval, connector review, and data classification.
Keep rollback, audit, and stale-data indicators visible.

knownPitfalls: Showing metrics without source, timestamp, or limitation.
Adding write controls before command intake, approvals, and audit are runtime-ready.
Connecting live data before repository-backed read-only value is proven.
Using decorative layouts that slow operational scanning.
approvalGates: Owner approval is required before live connectors, database access, deployment, domain or DNS changes, paid tools, or credentials.
Owner approval is required before displaying customer, production, confidential, or restricted data.
Owner approval is required before adding write buttons, forms, destructive actions, or command execution.
Owner approval is required before Netlify production deployment.
qualityChecklist: Every data surface has source, timestamp, and limitation context.
Dashboard remains read-only unless a separate approved command model exists.
Responsive layout is dense, scannable, accessible, and free of overlapping text.
Blocked actions, approvals, incidents, costs, and validation state are visible.
whatToBuildFirst: Read-only status overview from repository records.
Project, connector, command, cost, capability, watchdog, and memory panels.
Local dashboard build and check validation.
whatNotToOverbuildEarly: Live database, Netlify, n8n, or GitHub adapters before approval.
Write controls, command bars, or destructive actions.
Complex charting before source reliability is clear.
Role-based access beyond documented owner and future placeholders.
stopConditions: Stop before live connectors, deployment, domain or DNS, paid tools, credentials, production data, or customer data.
Stop before adding write actions, command execution, or destructive controls.
Stop before modifying Lead Gen production or the AI Receptionist repo.
notes: Archetype knowledge record only. It does not deploy or enable live dashboard actions.
